```python
expected_state = "101"
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
expected_counts = counts.get(expected_state, 0)
success_rate = expected_counts / total_counts
assert success_rate > 0.9, f"Expected state {expected_state} should dominate measurements, got {success_rate:.3f} success rate"
assert expected_counts == max(counts.values()), f"Expected state {expected_state} should be the most frequent measurement"
```

```python
expected_state = "101"
expected_count = counts.get(expected_state, 0)
total_other_counts = sum(count for state, count in counts.items() if state != expected_state)

# Verify that the expected state has the majority of measurements
assert expected_count > shots * 0.8, f"Expected state {expected_state} should dominate measurements, got {expected_count}/{shots}"

# Verify that other states have minimal counts (allowing for quantum noise)
assert total_other_counts < shots * 0.2, f"Other states should be minimal, got {total_other_counts}/{shots}"
```

```python
expected_state = "101"
total_expected_counts = counts.get(expected_state, 0)
total_other_counts = sum(count for state, count in counts.items() if state != expected_state)

# Verify that the expected state dominates the measurements
assert total_expected_counts > total_other_counts, f"Expected state '{expected_state}' should be the most frequent, but got {total_expected_counts} vs {total_other_counts} for others"

# Verify that the expected state appears in at least 80% of shots
expected_ratio = total_expected_counts / shots
assert expected_ratio >= 0.8, f"Expected state '{expected_state}' should appear in at least 80% of shots, but got {expected_ratio:.3f}"
```

```python
expected_state = "101"
expected_count = counts.get(expected_state, 0)
total_other_counts = sum(count for state, count in counts.items() if state != expected_state)

# Verify that the expected state dominates the measurements
assert expected_count > 0.8 * shots, f"Expected state {expected_state} appeared only {expected_count} times out of {shots} shots"

# Verify that other states are minimal (allowing for quantum noise/errors)
assert total_other_counts < 0.2 * shots, f"Unexpected states appeared {total_other_counts} times out of {shots} shots"
```

```python
expected_state = "101"
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
expected_counts = counts.get(expected_state, 0)
success_rate = expected_counts / total_counts if total_counts > 0 else 0

assert expected_state in counts, f"Expected state {expected_state} not found in measurement results"
```
